title: Ball Screw Whirling Machine
content:
![Ball Screw Whirling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-screw-whirling-machine-1-2-1_01_b.webp)

Ball Screw Whirling Machine

Designed for high-efficiency thread machining of long-shaft components such as ball screws and worm shafts, the high-precision CNC ball screw whirling machine is a dedicated solution for precision transmission manufacturing. Using high-speed whirling cutter head enveloping milling, threads are formed in a single pass, achieving high productivity with minimal grinding allowance and making the system well suited for batch production environments.

Widely adopted in the precision machining of ball screws, planetary roller screws, and large-lead transmission screws, this whirling system serves as a core piece of equipment for the mass production of precision transmission components.

|---|---|---|---|---|

Key Advantages

- Easy Operation: The control system is equipped with SIEMENS 828D/840D CNC platforms, combined with HEIDENHAIN/FAGOR precision linear scales and Renishaw encoders, enabling full closed-loop control.

- Intelligent Compensation: Features single-pass cyclic milling with automated capabilities for continuous machining, multi-start thread milling, dynamic tool setting, and precise lead/pitch diameter taper compensation.

- Stable Precision: Synchronous CNC control guarantees superior lead accuracy and profile consistency. Precisely controlled grinding allowances fully support C3/C5 grade ball screw process chains.

- High Flexibility: By changing the cutter head and adjusting the A-axis, complex thread forms such as multi-start threads, variable-lead threads, and planetary roller screws can be processed with ease.

- Green Machining: Supports dry cutting and minimum quantity lubrication (MQL), significantly reducing coolant consumption and environmental disposal costs.

- High Efficiency: Roughing and semi-finishing operations are completed in a single setup, delivering productivity levels 5-20 times higher than traditional turning methods and making the solution ideal for large-scale production.

content:
Planetary Screw Whirling Machine

![Planetary Screw Whirling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/planetary-screw-whirling-machine-1-2-2_01_b.webp)

Engineered specifically for the unique helical raceway structure of planetary roller screws, the planetary screw whirling machine is a dedicated CNC machining solution for high-precision thread and raceway forming. By utilizing high-speed whirling milling technology, it enables efficient and accurate forming of roller raceways and multi-start threads, serving as a critical piece of equipment for machining core transmission surfaces of planetary roller screws and meeting the stringent accuracy requirements of high-end equipment manufacturing.

The planetary screw whirling machine is widely used in industrial robot joint modules, aerospace servo mechanisms, new energy vehicle electric drive systems, and high-end CNC machine tools, making it a core machining solution for the mass production of high-precision planetary roller screws.

- High Machining Efficiency: Compared with conventional turning processes, productivity is increased by 8-25 times. Rough and semi-finish milling can be completed in a single setup, with grinding allowance precisely controlled within 0.05–0.15mm, significantly reducing subsequent grinding time.

- Excellent Precision Adaptability: Dedicated form cutters combined with multi-axis synchronous control enable accurate machining of multi-start and variable-lead raceways. Raceway cross-section accuracy and lead precision are far superior to those achieved by standard machining equipment.

title: Vertical Precision Lathe
content:
![Vertical Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/products/vertical-precision-lathe-1-3-2_01_b.webp)

Vertical Precision Lathe

A vertical precision lathe is a high-accuracy CNC machining system featuring a vertically oriented spindle and a horizontally rotating worktable. The workpiece is mounted on the rotary table, while cutting tools perform turning operations through vertical and horizontal feed movements. This configuration is primarily used for rough and finish machining of large, heavy, disc-shaped, and hub-type components, offering high automation, stable machining accuracy, and high production efficiency.

- Vertical Structural Layout: With the workpiece clamped in a vertical orientation, the gravity direction is aligned with the clamping direction, effectively preventing deformation caused by self-weight in large components. This results in superior clamping stability compared to horizontal lathes.

- High-Precision Machining: Positioning accuracy reaches 0.005-0.01mm, with repeat positioning accuracy ≤0.003mm, meeting precision machining requirements for faces, inner diameters, and outer diameters of disc-type components.

- Highly Automated Operation: Supports multi-process compound machining, including turning, milling, drilling, boring, and tapping. An optional automatic tool changer (ATC) enables unattended continuous operation, significantly reducing process transfer time.

- Heavy-Duty Machining Capability: The worktable load capacity ranges from several tons to several hundred tons. Workpieces with diameters exceeding 10m can be machined, making the system suitable for large-scale components in industries such as metallurgy and wind power.

- Flexible CNC Control: Multiple sets of machining parameters can be stored within the CNC system, allowing rapid program switching for different workpiece specifications and supporting small-batch, high-mix production requirements.

title: Vertical Compound Superfinishing Machine
content:
Vertical Compound Superfinishing Machine

![Vertical Compound Superfinishing Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/vertical-compound-superfinishing-machine-1-5-1_01_b.webp)

The vertical compound superfinishing machine is a core device used in the final stage of bearing manufacturing. It uses fine-grit abrasives (such as oilstones) to perform micro-cutting and polishing on raceways and grooves of bearing rings. The main function is to reduce surface roughness, eliminate waviness, create residual compressive stress, and enhance the bearing's fatigue life and NVH (Noise, Vibration, and Harshness) performance, all while maintaining the workpiece's shape accuracy. It is widely used for ultra-precision grinding of large bearing rings, such as automotive bearing rings, wind power and rail transport large rings, and thin-walled spindle rings for machine tools.

Technical Specifications

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

Key Advantages

- High-Frequency Oscillation and Micro-Feed Compound Process: By using a compound motion combining micro-cutting and polishing, surface roughness of the raceway can be reduced to Ra ≤ 0.02 μm.

- Eliminates Grinding-Induced Tensile Stress: This process effectively enhances the bearing's lifespan by eliminating tensile stress generated during grinding.

- Advanced Oil Film Technology: Minimizes precision loss by mitigating thermal deformation and stress concentration through precision oil film technology.

- Enhanced Throughput: Optimizes both auxiliary and superfinishing cycles to deliver higher production efficiency and shorter lead times.

- Customizable Range: The machining range can be customized according to customer needs, making it adaptable for processing thin-walled bearing rings of various sizes and technical requirements.

- Enhanced Kinematic Performance: For ball screws, the smooth raceways ensure stable ball movement, reducing vibration and noise, while improving motion precision and positioning stability.

- Enhanced Corrosion Resistance: The smooth surface prevents the accumulation of dirt and moisture, improving rust resistance and corrosion resistance.

- Spiral Trajectory Control: Features advanced CNC spiral trajectory tracking that precisely synchronizes with the screw lead and helix angle, ensuring uniform finishing across both the thread flanks and crests.

title: Pipe Beveling Machine
content:
![Pipe Beveling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/pipe-beveling-machine-1-7_01_b.webp)

Pipe Beveling Machine

The pipe beveling machine is a specialized piece of equipment used to process specific angle bevels on the end faces or inner walls of metal pipes. Its main purpose is to ensure uniform weld penetration and meet joint strength standards during pipe welding. It is widely used in industries such as petrochemical, natural gas transportation, municipal pipelines, and shipbuilding for beveling pipes before welding.

Technical Specifications

| Model | PB600 | PB900 |
|---|---|---|
| Applicable Pipe Material | carbon steel, stainless steel, alloy steel | carbon steel, stainless steel, alloy steel |
| Beveling Profile | Single V, Double V, U Type | Single V, Double V, U Type |
| Drive | Electric | Electric |
| Applicable Pipe Diameter | Ø305-Ø600mm | Ø600-Ø900mm |
| Applicable Pipe Wall Thickness | 10-150mm | 10-120mm |
| Pipe Clamping Method | Hydraulic clamping (automatic centering) | Hydraulic clamping (automatic centering) |
| Beveling Angle | 60-70° | 60-70° |
| Root Face Height | 1-3mm | 1-3mm |
| Automatic Feed Rate | 0.2-0.6mm/r | 0.2-0.6mm/r |
| Dimensions (L×W×H) | 2400×1400×1300mm | 2400×1700×1650mm |

Key Advantages

- Outstanding Machining Quality: The beveling machine primarily uses cold cutting, ensuring precise, distortion-free cuts with no heat-affected zone. The smooth, burr-free surface provides a perfect pre-treatment for high-quality welding.

- Excellent Environmental Adaptability: The design of the beveler takes field requirements into full consideration. With a portable or split design, the equipment can be taken directly to any position on the pipeline, offering strong adaptability.

- High Integration and Automation: The beveler integrates functions like cutting and beveling in one system, allowing multiple processes to be completed in a single setup. It uses an advanced integrated CNC system that can automatically perform complex beveling operations in batches via programming, with features like automatic alignment and tool feeding.

- Significant Economic Benefits: Reduces labor costs, saves auxiliary materials, and lowers overall costs.

title: PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)
content:
PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)

PCBN (Polycrystalline Cubic Boron Nitride) is a superhard material with hardness second only to diamond. It is sintered from CBN micron powder and metal or ceramic binders under high temperature and high pressure. Hardness typically ranges from HV 2500 to 5000. It is often composited with cemented carbide to increase strength, enabling efficient processing.

PCBN tools are used for the machining of hard ferrous metals to improve efficiency, precision, and stability while reducing comprehensive costs. PCBN tools are also suitable for automated production lines and dry cutting applications.

PCBN is a primary material for difficult-to-machine materials. Application areas include: hardened steel, cast iron, high-temperature alloys, and powder metallurgy.

Features

- Hardness and Wear Resistance: PCBN hardness is higher than cemented carbide and ceramic tools. It resists cutting wear of hard materials such as hardened steel (above HRC 60) and chilled cast iron. Tool life is 5 to 15 times that of cemented carbide tools, reducing tool change frequency and production costs.

- Thermal Stability: Heat resistance reaches 1400-1500°C. Performance remains stable in high-temperature cutting environments, supporting dry cutting and high-speed cutting. This reduces cutting fluid usage and environmental impact.

- Chemical Inertness: No chemical reaction occurs with ferrous materials at 1200-1300°C. This prevents bonding and diffusion between the tool and workpiece, ensuring processing accuracy and surface quality in ferrous metal machining.

- Friction Coefficient: The friction coefficient is 0.1-0.3. Cutting forces and temperatures are lower compared to cemented carbide, reducing tool-chip sticking while increasing processing efficiency.

- Thermal Conductivity: High thermal conductivity allows for rapid dissipation of cutting heat. This reduces thermal deformation and improves dimensional accuracy for heat-sensitive materials.

- Processing Adaptability: Suitable for roughing, semi-finishing, and finishing operations. The hard turning process increases efficiency by 3 to 10 times compared to grinding. Applications include the automotive, aerospace, and mold industries.

- Edge Retention: The cutting edge maintains its initial geometry during operation. This ensures processing consistency for automated production and high-precision requirements.

title: PCD Tools
content:
PCD Tools

(PCD Turning Tool, PCD Milling Tool, PCD Boring Tool, PCD Grooving Tool, PCD Threading Tool)

PCD tools are superhard tools designed by sintering diamond micron powder with a binder phase using High Pressure and High Temperature (HPHT) processes. With hardness second only to diamond, PCD exhibits high wear resistance, a low friction coefficient, and high thermal conductivity. It is primarily used for precision machining of non-ferrous metals and non-metallic materials. It is particularly efficient for processing high-silicon aluminum alloys, pure aluminum, and copper alloys.

Selection Guide for PCD Turning Tools

| Image | Model | Edge Length | Thickness | Hole Diameter | Details |

| | VCGW | 6.35-9.525mm | 3.18-4.76mm | 2.8-4.4mm | View Details |

For more models, please feel free to .

Features

- Hardness and wear resistance: Hardness reaches HV 8000-10000, which is significantly higher than cemented carbide tools. When machining high-silicon aluminum alloys and carbon fiber reinforced polymers (CFRP), tool life is 50 to 100 times that of cemented carbide, reducing tool change frequency.

- Low friction and anti-bonding properties: PCD has a low affinity for non-ferrous metals such as aluminum and copper. This prevents built-up edge formation during cutting, allowing for surface quality below Ra 0.1 μm and preventing workpiece surface scratches.

- High thermal conductivity: Thermal conductivity is 5 to 10 times higher than that of cemented carbide. Cutting heat dissipates rapidly through the tool body, reducing thermal deformation and supporting high-speed cutting conditions.

FMP-BE Indexable Milling Cutters

Indexable milling cutters are primarily used for high-speed face and shoulder milling of non-ferrous metals. They offer exceptional versatility for both roughing and finishing operations, significantly reducing downtime and optimizing production costs.

 CNC electrophoretic magnetic chuck is an advanced magnetic fixture that combines the characteristics of electromagnets and permanent magnets. It utilizes electrical pulses to control the connection of the magnetic circuit. During operation, it maintains a strong magnetic force without a continuous power supply. title: Processing Solutions for Ball Nuts & Planetary Nuts
content:
Processing Solutions for Ball Nuts & Planetary Nuts

![Processing Solutions for Ball Nuts & Planetary Nuts](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-nuts-planetary-nuts-applications_01b.webp)

The nut is the core moving component of the screw assembly. It is critical for equipment requiring high-precision, heavy-load linear displacement control. The finishing of the internal nut raceway is the most demanding step; it requires a strict balance of profile accuracy, surface roughness, and mass-production stability to ensure smooth engagement with the screw.

Process Flow

Bar Stock Preparation

Soft Roughing

Induction Hardening

Internal Hard Turning / Elbow Grinding

Key Process Solutions

Induction Heat Treatment

The Challenge

- Rigidity & Vibration: Machining deep internal bores often leads to tool chatter due to poor equipment rigidity, compromising the surface finish.

- Precision Control: Maintaining tight dimensional and geometric tolerances inside a hardened nut is notoriously difficult.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Horizontal Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/horizontal-precision-lathe-1-3-1.html)

![Horizontal Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/products/horizontal-precision-lathe-1-3-1_01b.webp)

- "Turning Instead of Grinding": This machine is engineered for high-precision hard turning. It replaces traditional internal grinding, allowing for the complete machining of complex geometries in a single setup (clamping).

- Sub-Micron Accuracy: The machine rigidity allows for process stability that rivals grinding. It achieves sub-micron level precision and superior surface quality directly on hardened workpieces.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)](https://winicetech-en.com/pcbn-cutting-tools-2-2.html)

![PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-nuts-planetary-nuts-applications_02.webp)

- Performance: Our PCBN inserts feature exceptional wear resistance and razor-sharp cutting edges. They are essential for maintaining the surface roughness requirements of the internal raceway over long production runs.

Elbow Grinding

The Challenge

- Interference: When grinding nuts with small deep holes or large helix angles (large lead), a standard straight grinding quill will physically collide (interfere) with the thread flank due to the steep lead angle.

- Deformation: Thin-walled nuts are highly sensitive to clamping forces and cutting pressure, leading to ovality or distortion during processing.

Our Solution

 Recommended Cutting Tools: [Elbow Grinding Whirling Tools](https://winicetech-en.com/elbow-grinding-whirling-tools-2-6.html)

![Elbow Grinding Whirling Tools](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-nuts-planetary-nuts-applications_03.webp)

- Geometry: Specifically designed for internal thread grinding of special nuts. The "Elbow" (bent) design aligns the grinding wheel with the helix angle, eliminating interference issues common with large-lead nuts.

- Advantage: Compared to standard interference grinding, the elbow grinding whirling tool offers significantly higher stability, a consistent contact angle, and superior surface roughness.

title: Precision Ball Screw Machining Solutions
content:
Precision Ball Screw Machining Solutions

![Precision Ball Screw Machining Solutions](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-screws-applications_01b.webp)

The ball screw is the "heart" of precision transmission. It acts as a high-efficiency mechanical drive that converts rotary motion into linear motion. By fundamentally replacing "sliding friction" with "rolling friction," ball screws achieve superior transmission efficiency, micron-level precision, and an extended service life. As a core functional component, they are indispensable in CNC machine tools, industrial robots, precision instruments, automotive manufacturing, and the aerospace industry.

Process Flow

Bar Stock Preparation

Soft Roughing

Induction Hardening

Straightening

Hard Whirling

Straightening

Precision Thread Grinding

Polishing

Key Process Solutions

Induction Heat Treatment

The Challenge

- Hardness: Uneven quenching depths in both circumferential and longitudinal directions lead to machining inconsistencies and lead deviations. Furthermore, insufficient tempering results in dimensional instability.

- Deformation Risks: Long, slender screws are highly prone to bending during quenching. Subsequent straightening induces residual stress, which gradually releases during long-term use, causing a loss of precision.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Horizontal Induction Hardening Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/horizontal-induction-hardening-machine-1-1-1.html)

![Horizontal Induction Hardening Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/horizontal-induction-hardening-machine-1-1-1_01b.webp)

- Horizontal Layout: Specifically designed to accommodate long ball screw workpieces.

- Induction Heating: The machine inputs Medium or High-Frequency current to generate an alternating magnetic field. This induces eddy currents on the screw surface, causing it to rapidly heat to the quenching temperature (850–900°C).

- Precise Quenching: Immediate spray cooling transforms the surface into a high-hardness Martensite structure while the core retains its toughness. This ensures uniform heating even on complex raceway profiles.

Hard Whirling

The Challenge

- Precision Limitations: Traditional methods rely on multiple disjointed steps, leading to cumulative pitch errors that fail to meet the "micron-level" transmission demands of high-end equipment.

- Low Efficiency: A conventional workflow involves turning followed by multiple grinding passes. This is cumbersome and slow, with cycle times reaching 8–12 hours per screw, creating massive pressure on production delivery.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Ball Screw Whirling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/ball-screw-whirling-machine-1-2-1.html)

![Ball Screw Whirling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-screw-whirling-machine-1-2-1_01b.webp)

- Features a high-precision CNC system paired with custom PCBN tooling to perform hard whirling directly on heat-treated workpieces.

- Dry Cutting Capable: The process is stable and energy-efficient, capable of achieving a raceway surface roughness of Ra 0.4 μm in typical conditions without the need for coolant.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [PCBN Tools (Tool Bits for Whirling)](https://winicetech-en.com/pcbn-tools-2-1.html)

![PCBN Tools (Tool Bits for Whirling)](https://winicetech-en.com/products/ball-screws-applications_02.webp)

- We provide a variety of PCBN grades and edge structures designed for screws made of GCr15, 4150, and other materials heat-treated to HRC 58–62.

- Ensures high precision and dimensional consistency. Specialized PCBN grades are also available for difficult-to-cut materials like stainless steel.

Thread Grinding

The Challenge

- Workpiece Deformation: Long screws tend to sag or bend under the combined effects of gravity and cutting forces, compromising straightness.

- Thermal Instability: Heat generated during grinding causes thermal expansion. Precise control over cutting parameters and cooling is required to prevent errors.

- Surface Integrity: Achieving ultra-low surface roughness requires the rigorous selection of grinding wheels, parameters, and lubrication.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [CNC High Precision Thread Grinding Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/cnc-high-precision-thread-grinding-machine-1-4.html)

![CNC High Precision Thread Grinding Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/cnc-high-precision-thread-grinding-machine-1-4_01b.webp)

- Utilizes single-line or form grinding wheels to perform precision grinding along the screw’s helix.

- Delivers the perfect balance of high precision and high efficiency for final finishing.

Polishing

The Challenge

- Maintaining Precision: Poor polishing can inadvertently alter the thread lead and tooth profile accuracy.

- Surface Inconsistency: It is difficult to uniformly polish the root, crest, and flanks, leading to scratches, micro-cracks, or uneven roughness.

- Efficiency vs. Accuracy: Manual polishing is slow and inconsistent, while improper mechanical polishing generates heat that affects the screw's rigidity and lifespan.

- Complex Geometries: Polishing is complicated by variable lead angles, helix lifts, and special specifications like large leads or fine threads.

Our Solution

- Uses an elastic polishing head that performs both polishing and lapping.

- Automatically matches the screw’s lead and helix angle to ensure uniform polishing across the tooth flank and crest.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [Flexible Polishing Head](https://winicetech-en.com/flexible-polishing-head-2-7.html)

title: Integrated Bearing Roller Machining & Superfinishing Solutions
content:
Integrated Bearing Roller Machining & Superfinishing Solutions

![Integrated Bearing Roller Machining & Superfinishing Solutions](https://winicetech-en.com/products/bearing-rollers-applications_01b.webp)

Bearing rollers are the primary load-bearing elements of rolling bearings. Known for their high load capacity and impact resistance, they are critical components in machine tool spindles and heavy mechanical equipment. The core objective of roller manufacturing is to achieve the "The Three Pillars of Precision" of precision: dimensional consistency, geometric accuracy, and surface quality. Precise control across all processes ensures efficient meshing with the bearing rings, significantly enhancing rotation accuracy and service life.

Process Flow

Small Rollers (<50mm)

Material Preparation

Soft Turning (or Cold Heading)

Heat Treatment (Through-Hardening or Carburizing)

Face Grinding

Centerless Grinding

Superfinishing

Large Rollers (≥50mm):

Material Preparation

Soft Turning

Heat Treatment

Hard Turning

Superfinishing

Key Process Solutions

Hard Turning

The Challenge

- Material Difficulty: High hardness results in extreme cutting forces and temperatures, leading to rapid tool wear and potential "grinding burns" on the workpiece.

- Geometric Control: Rollers require strict cylindricity and runout tolerances. Improper clamping can cause deformation or positioning skew, leading to excessive runout at the chamfers.

- Consistency: Maintaining batch stability during mass production of large hardened rollers is technically demanding.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Horizontal Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/horizontal-precision-lathe-1-3-1.html)

![Horizontal Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/products/horizontal-precision-lathe-1-3-1_01b.webp)

- "Turning Instead of Grinding": This CNC machine is engineered for high-precision hard turning. It allows for multi-step processing in a single setup (clamping).

- Sub-Micron Precision: The rigid structure enables the machine to achieve sub-micron level accuracy and superior surface quality, effectively replacing the need for rough grinding operations.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)](https://winicetech-en.com/pcbn-cutting-tools-2-2.html)

![PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)](https://winicetech-en.com/products/bearing-rollers-applications_02.webp)

- Performance: Our PCBN tools feature exceptional wear resistance and sharp cutting edges, ensuring the surface roughness remains within tolerance throughout the production batch.

Superfinishing

The Challenge

- Profile Control: Precisely generating the logarithmic crown profile (convexity) is difficult but essential to prevent edge stress concentration in the bearing.

- Surface Consistency: In mass production, maintaining consistent surface texture and roughness across thousands of rollers is a major hurdle.

- Vibration Defects: Insufficient rigidity in the clamping or oscillation mechanism can introduce vibration marks (chatter) or dimensional fluctuations, ruining the surface finish.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Horizontal Compound Superfinishing Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/horizontal-compound-superfinishing-machine-1-5-2.html)

![Horizontal Compound Superfinishing Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/horizontal-compound-superfinishing-machine-1-5-2_01b.webp)

- Plunge-Cut Technology: Utilizes a "Plunge-Cut" mechanism combined with auxiliary superfinishing processes.

- Surface Tribology: The core objective is to reduce surface roughness and remove amorphous grinding layers. It creates a cross-hatch pattern that forms uniform oil retention grooves, significantly improving lubrication capability and wear resistance.

title: Precision Machining Solutions for Large-Scale Bearing Rings
content:
Precision Machining Solutions for Large-Scale Bearing Rings

![Precision Machining Solutions for Large-Scale Bearing Rings](https://winicetech-en.com/products/large-scale-bearing-rings-applications_01b.webp)

Large bearing rings (typically defined as having an inner diameter ≥ 100mm) are the critical load-bearing components of heavy-duty bearings. A complete set includes an inner ring and an outer ring, which support the rolling elements and guide rotation. These components must meet rigorous standards for roundness, cylindricity, and surface roughness. They are the backbone of heavy equipment, new energy (wind power), rail transit, and marine engineering.

Process Flow

Material Preparation

Soft Turning

Flaw Detection (NDT)

Heat Treatment

Face Grinding (or Hard Turning)

Hard Turning

Precision Grinding

Superfinishing (as required)

Key Process Solutions

Hard Turning

The Challenge

- Deformation & Tolerance: Large rings have strict requirements for runout and roundness. However, the heat generated during hard turning, combined with uneven clamping forces from traditional jaws, often causes thermal deformation and dimensional errors.

- Thin-Wall Instability: Many large bearing rings feature thin walls. The cutting force can distort the ring during machining; once the clamps are released, the ring "springs back," destroying the machined precision (roundness).

- Vibration (Chatter): Machining large diameters is prone to chatter (vibration marks), which ruins the surface finish and drastically reduces tool life. This demands exceptional machine rigidity.

- Tool Wear: With hardness levels between HRC 58–62, standard tools wear out rapidly, leading to high consumable costs and frequent downtime for tool changes.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Vertical Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/vertical-precision-lathe-1-3-2.html)

![Vertical Precision Lathe](https://winicetech-en.com/products/vertical-precision-lathe-1-3-2_01b.webp)

- Vertical Stability: The vertical layout uses gravity to help seat the large workpiece, improving stability compared to horizontal lathes.

- Clamping Innovation: The machine utilizes a hydraulic chuck with soft jaws for even force distribution. For sensitive thin-walled rings, it can be equipped with expansion mandrels or electro-permanent magnetic chucks to completely eliminate physical clamping distortion.

- Capability: Capable of roughing and finishing internal/external raceways, ribs, shoulders, and grooves in a single setup.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)](https://winicetech-en.com/pcbn-cutting-tools-2-2.html)

![PCBN Cutting Tools (Tool Bits for Turning)](https://winicetech-en.com/products/large-scale-bearing-rings-applications_02.webp)

- Performance: High-grade PCBN tools provide the necessary wear resistance and impact strength to maintain consistent surface roughness on hardened steel, significantly reducing tool change frequency.

Superfinishing

The Challenge

- Process Synergy: Achieving the perfect "mirror finish" requires precise coordination of stone grit, pressure, feed rate, and oscillation frequency. On large surface areas, uneven stone wear can lead to uncontrolled roughness or waviness.

- Surface Consistency: Due to the large circumference, maintaining uniform contact pressure is difficult. This often results in "over-polishing" or "under-polishing" in different sectors, creating uneven residual stress distribution that affects bearing life.

- Clamping Distortion: Large rings are relatively flexible. If clamped too tightly, they deform; if too loose, they slip. Furthermore, any instability in the oscillation mechanism will transfer vibration marks to the raceway.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Vertical Compound Superfinishing Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/vertical-compound-superfinishing-machine-1-5-1.html)

![Vertical Compound Superfinishing Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/vertical-compound-superfinishing-machine-1-5-1_01b.webp)

- Magnetic Holding: This machine features an electro-permanent magnetic chuck. By holding the ring magnetically rather than mechanically, it allows for distortion-free processing of the entire raceway.

- Micro-Cutting: Uses fine-grit oil stones to perform micro-cutting and polishing on raceways and grooves.

- Result: Improves the surface geometry and material fatigue life, preparing the bearing for heavy-duty operation.

title: Precision Pipe Beveling & End Preparation Solutions
content:
Precision Pipe Beveling & End Preparation Solutions

![Precision Pipe Beveling & End Preparation Solutions](https://winicetech-en.com/products/pipe-beveling-applications_01b.webp)

Pipe beveling is a critical pre-processing step in industrial welding. It involves machining the pipe end into a specific geometric angle to form a groove. This preparation ensures that the welding arc can penetrate deep into the root of the pipe wall, guaranteeing full weld fusion and maximizing joint strength. By creating a precise bevel, we eliminate common defects such as "lack of penetration" and "false welding," ensuring the structural integrity of the pipeline.

Widely Applied In

- Oil & Gas: Long-distance transmission pipelines.

- Industrial Systems: Chemical plants, nuclear power stations, shipbuilding, and water treatment facilities.

- Infrastructure: Urban pipe networks and municipal engineering projects.

Key Process Solutions

The Challenge

- Dimensional Control on Large Pipes: For large-diameter pipes (especially those >1 meter), maintaining geometric accuracy is difficult. Inherent ovality or uneven wall thickness often leads to inconsistent bevel angles and uneven root faces (blunt edges), making automated welding impossible.

- Material Toughness: Special materials like high-strength alloy steels and stainless steel are difficult to cut. They cause rapid tool wear and slow down production efficiency due to their high hardness and toughness.

- Surface Cleanliness: The bevel face must be bright metal—completely free of oil, oxidation, and rust. Any contamination directly compromises the quality of the final weld.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Pipe Beveling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/pipe-beveling-machine-1-7.html)

![Pipe Beveling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/pipe-beveling-machine-1-7_01b.webp)

- Automation: Select the appropriate CNC model based on your specific pipe diameter, wall thickness, and material.

- Self-Centering: Features robust hydraulic chucks that automatically clamp and center the pipe, correcting minor ovality.

- Efficiency: The machine performs automatic machining without the need for constant manual supervision, delivering a perfectly machined surface ready for welding.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [Pipe Beveling Tools](https://winicetech-en.com/pipe-beveling-tools-2-4.html)

title: Precision Planetary Roller Screw Machining Solutions
content:
Precision Planetary Roller Screw Machining Solutions

![Precision Planetary Roller Screw Machining Solutions](https://winicetech-en.com/products/planetary-screws-applications_01b.webp)

Planetary roller screws are high-precision, heavy-load mechanical transmission devices composed of a screw, a nut, and planetary rollers. Unlike ball screws planetary screws utilize "line contact" between the rollers and the screw. This fundamental difference results in significantly higher load-bearing capacity, superior rigidity, stronger shock resistance, and a longer service life within a compact footprint. They are the preferred choice for aerospace, heavy industry, energy sectors, and high-force industrial robotics.

Process Flow

Bar Stock Preparation

Soft Roughing

Induction Heat Treatment

Hard Whirling / Precision Grinding

Polishing

Key Process Solutions

Induction Heat Treatment

The Challenge

- Deformation Control: Planetary screws often feature complex multi-start threads and slender structures. This makes them extremely susceptible to bending, leading to lead errors, pitch deviations, and straightness issues during heating.

- Stability Issues: Maintaining dimensional stability and controlling hardness uniformity across the entire length is difficult with traditional methods.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Vertical Induction Hardening Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/vertical-induction-hardening-machine-1-1-2.html)

![Vertical Induction Hardening Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/vertical-induction-hardening-machine-1-1-2_01b.webp)

- Vertical Architecture: Features a vertical layout specifically designed to minimize gravitational distortion during the heating of long screw shafts.

- Induction Physics: The machine inputs medium or high-frequency current into the coil to generate an alternating magnetic field. This induces eddy currents on the screw surface, causing it to rapidly heat to the quenching temperature (850–900°C).

- Precise Quenching: Immediate spray cooling transforms the surface into a high-hardness Martensite structure while the core retains its toughness. This ensures uniform heating even on the complex multi-start profiles required for planetary screws.

Hard Whirling

The Challenge

- Accumulated Errors: Traditional processing relies on multiple disjointed steps. This results in cumulative pitch errors that cannot meet the "micron-level" transmission requirements of high-end equipment.

- Production Bottlenecks: The conventional workflow (turning + multi-pass grinding) is inefficient. A single screw can take 8–12 hours to process, making mass production and timely delivery nearly impossible.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [Planetary Screw Whirling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/planetary-screw-whirling-machine-1-2-2.html)

![Planetary Screw Whirling Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/planetary-screw-whirling-machine-1-2-2_01b.webp)

- Process: Features a high-precision CNC system paired with custom PCBN tooling to perform hard whirling directly on heat-treated workpieces.

- Performance: Supports dry cutting (no coolant needed). The process is stable and energy-efficient, capable of achieving a raceway surface roughness of Ra 0.4 μm in typical conditions.

Recommended Cutting Tools: [PCBN Tools (Tool Bits for Whirling)](https://winicetech-en.com/pcbn-tools-2-1.html)

![PCBN Tools (Tool Bits for Whirling)](https://winicetech-en.com/products/planetary-screws-applications_02.webp)

- We provide specialized PCBN grades and edge structures designed for screws made of GCr15, 4150, and other materials heat-treated to HRC 58–62.

- Versatility: Ensures high precision and dimensional consistency, with specific grades available for difficult-to-cut materials like stainless steel.

Thread Grinding

The Challenge

- Workpiece Deformation: Long screws are prone to bending under the combined effects of gravity and cutting forces, which compromises straightness.

- Thermal Growth: Heat generated during grinding causes the workpiece to expand. Precise control over cutting parameters and cooling is essential to prevent dimensional errors.

- Surface Quality: Meeting the strict surface roughness requirements for planetary interaction requires rigorous selection of grinding wheels and lubrication.

Our Solution

 Recommended Equipment: [CNC High-Precision Thread Grinding Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/cnc-high-precision-thread-grinding-machine-1-4.html)

![CNC High Precision Thread Grinding Machine](https://winicetech-en.com/products/cnc-high-precision-thread-grinding-machine-1-4_01b.webp)

- Precision Control: Utilizes single-line or form grinding wheels to perform precision grinding along the screw’s helix.

- Outcome: Delivers the perfect balance of high precision and high efficiency, ensuring the screw meets the rigorous geometric tolerances required for planetary roller integration.
